summary.headline: Best opportunity today: productized MTD-for-Income-Tax onboarding and automation, with late-payment and AI admin services close behind.
summary.best_opportunity: Making Tax Digital onboarding, records cleanup and lightweight filing automation for sole traders, landlords and small accountancy firms
summary.why_now: HMRC's live GOV.UK guidance says sole traders and landlords over £50,000 qualifying income must use Making Tax Digital for Income Tax from 6 April 2026, with the £30,000 threshold following from 6 April 2027. That gives a hard compliance trigger, fragmented buyers and a service-first route to fast cash plus recurring support.

top_opportunities.name: Making Tax Digital onboarding and automation for sole traders, landlords and accountants
top_opportunities.description: Sell a fixed-scope offer that cleans up records, connects bookkeeping software, sets reminder workflows and provides recurring quarterly support for MTD for Income Tax.
top_opportunities.why_now: Current GOV.UK guidance is unusually clear: qualifying income above £50,000 enters MTD for Income Tax from 6 April 2026, above £30,000 from 6 April 2027 and above £20,000 from 6 April 2028. That creates a real near-term compliance deadline rather than speculative demand.
top_opportunities.buyer: Sole traders, small landlords, bookkeepers, small accountancy practices
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: High - setup fee plus monthly or quarterly support
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Excellent for a solo engineer because the first version is workflow setup, integrations and client dashboards rather than a full accounting product.
risks: Recognised accounting software brands already own mindshare, so the wedge must be migration and admin reduction rather than generic bookkeeping software
The offer must stay on software setup and process automation, not drift into regulated tax advice
top_opportunities.verdict: Best overall right now because urgency is real, buyers are numerous and a service-first launch can produce revenue quickly.

top_opportunities.name: Invoice chasing and e-invoicing automation for UK SMEs
top_opportunities.description: Build a productized service or micro-SaaS that sends cleaner invoices, automates reminders, tracks payment promises and gives owners a simple overdue-cash dashboard.
top_opportunities.why_now: The Small Business Commissioner homepage currently says late payments cost the UK economy £11 billion per year, highlights an interest calculator for overdue invoices and points small businesses toward e-invoicing guidance. That is a live cashflow pain with obvious ROI, even if the exact spend willingness varies by niche.
top_opportunities.buyer: Micro businesses, agencies, consultancies, trades firms, small B2B wholesalers and service companies
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: High - monthly usage or managed reminders service
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Very strong for a solo engineer because this is mostly integrations, reminders, templates and reporting, not deep domain product complexity.
risks: Some buyers will see this as a feature of accounting software unless the offer is tightly sold around reduced debtor days
Access to accounting data can be messy across different bookkeeping stacks
top_opportunities.verdict: One of the best fast-cash opportunities because the value proposition is immediate and measurable.

top_opportunities.name: AI lead-response and admin automation for trades and local service firms
top_opportunities.description: Productize missed-call text-back, instant web-form replies, quote follow-up, scheduling handoff and simple reporting for firms that lose work through slow response times.
top_opportunities.why_now: ONS' latest Business Insights bulletin says cost of labour was the most reported challenge for businesses with 10 or more employees in June 2026 at 38%. Combined with the government's AI Opportunities Action Plan pushing AI adoption across the UK, that supports a practical sell: use automation to protect revenue and reduce office admin. Evidence is solid on pain, but weaker on exactly which local niches will buy fastest.
top_opportunities.buyer: Plumbers, electricians, HVAC firms, clinics, salons, cleaning firms and other local service operators
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Strong for a solo engineer because the stack is mostly messaging, CRM glue, forms and dashboards.
risks: There is visible competition, so a narrow niche and ROI framing are essential
Some local firms have messy lead tracking, which makes proving value harder unless you instrument the funnel
top_opportunities.verdict: Still a strong opportunity, especially if sold as recovered jobs and lower admin time rather than generic AI consulting.

top_opportunities.name: SME tender-monitoring and bid-drafting assistant for public sector work
top_opportunities.description: Create a niche service or tool that monitors relevant tenders, summarises fit, drafts first-pass answers and keeps a reusable evidence library for small suppliers.
top_opportunities.why_now: The live Cabinet Office and HM Treasury SME Action Plan for 2025 to 2028 says the Procurement Act 2023 creates a simpler and more transparent regime, and departments now have SME targets and action plans to reduce bidding barriers. That is a timely signal that more SMEs will at least consider public sector work, though adoption will likely be strongest in a few verticals rather than across all small firms.
top_opportunities.buyer: Small consultancies, digital agencies, specialist service firms and compliance-heavy B2B suppliers
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: Medium to high - retainer, seat fee or managed bid support
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Good for an engineer who can combine scraping, search, document generation and workflow tooling into a very focused offer.
risks: Sales cycles can be slower than local-service automation because tendering is episodic
Bid-writing agencies and procurement consultancies already exist, so the offer needs automation leverage or a strong niche
top_opportunities.verdict: Promising niche B2B play, especially if you focus on one supplier type and sell time saved on each bid.

top_opportunities.name: Cyber Essentials readiness and security workflow automation for small organisations
top_opportunities.description: Offer a fixed-scope package covering backups, device hygiene, account security checklists, staff prompts and evidence collection for small organisations that need a cleaner security baseline.
top_opportunities.why_now: The NCSC small organisations guide was reviewed on 9 April 2026 and remains actively positioned for small and medium sized organisations and self-employed buyers. That makes cyber hygiene a credible productized service opportunity, especially as a practical operations package rather than broad security consultancy.
top_opportunities.buyer: Small professional firms, charities, clinics, local agencies and B2B service SMEs
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: Medium to high - monitoring, updates and periodic reviews
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Good if you like structured technical work and can keep the offer narrow, checklist-driven and implementation focused.
risks: Trust is important in security work, so some buyers may prefer established specialists
If the offer becomes too bespoke, part-time solo delivery gets harder
top_opportunities.verdict: Viable, but best as a tightly scoped operational service rather than an open-ended cyber consultancy.

best_pick.name: Making Tax Digital onboarding and automation for sole traders, landlords and accountants
best_pick.why_it_wins: It has the clearest hard deadline, the most obvious path to first revenue and the easiest service-first launch within a £2,000 budget. You can start by selling migration, setup and reminder workflows, then layer in recurring support and only later build proprietary software where patterns repeat.
validation_plan: Pick one wedge first: landlords with simple self-assessment needs or small accountancy firms that need an MTD migration helper for clients.
Build a minimal demo showing document capture, quarterly reminders and a simple client-status dashboard.
Publish a fixed-price MTD readiness package with clear scope, turnaround and monthly support options.
Contact 30 to 50 accountants, bookkeepers and landlord-facing businesses before building anything beyond onboarding and reporting.
best_pick.first_offer: MTD readiness setup for sole traders and landlords: records cleanup, software connection, reminder workflows and first-quarter support. Charge roughly £299 to £799 setup plus £39 to £149 per month depending on complexity.
best_pick.mvp: A thin internal tool plus off-the-shelf bookkeeping software, document capture, reminder automation and a small dashboard for onboarding status and quarterly check-ins.
customer_types: Sole traders over the MTD threshold
best_pick.do_today: Create a one-page MTD readiness offer, build a simple dashboard mock-up and start direct outreach to local accountants and landlord-focused businesses today.
